bundesagentur für arbeit english reha team agentur für arbeit aalen

The German Arbeitsagentur, commonly referred to as the Jobcenter in English, serves as a pivotal institution for job seekers and employees alike in Germany. Established under the Federal Employment Agency (Bundesagentur für Arbeit), the Jobcentre plays a crucial role in the country’s labor market, overseeing a wide range of services from job placement to unemployment benefits. All you need to know about admission to the German labour market. Working in Germany: step by step You are interested in working or undergoing a vocational training programme in Germany? We explain the requirements and what you need to do – step by step. Sie haben eine Frage oder ein Anliegen? Dann nutzen Sie unser Kontaktformular. Wir kümmern uns schnellstmöglich um Ihr Anliegen. Your entries in this form will be sent to the Federal Employment Agency via a secure internet connection (SSL encryption). As a rule, we will answer your request by post, unless you agree to a reply by e-mail. If your matters are about data protection-relevant information, we will answer you in each case by post. For this reason, we ask you to provide your address data. For technical reasons All you have to know about live, study,work in Germany. All information for people from other countries. Please select a concern from the field "Unemployment and finding work" Topics can be selected with the arrow keys The Bundesagentur für Arbeit (BA for short, AA or Arbeitsagentur) (' Federal Employment Agency') is a German federal agency under the Federal Ministry for Labour and Social Affairs. Its headquarters are in Nuremberg. Its director is Andrea Nahles. [1] The BA manages job centres across Germany and administers unemployment benefits. The agency is further divided into Arbeitsagentur and Jobcenter. Mission, services and organisation of the German Federal Employment Agency The Bundesagentur für Arbeit, Germany's Federal Employment Agency, is in charge of this online portal, which is intended to assist people in locating and applying to a variety of educational and training opportunities around the nation. Learn the translation for ‘Bundesagentur für Arbeit’ in LEO’s ­English ⇔ German­ dictionary. With noun/verb tables for the different cases and tenses links to audio pronunciation and relevant forum discussions free vocabulary trainer Advice and support You live outside of Germany. You would like to find a job or undergo vocational training in Germany. The Federal Employment Agency can help you. Make use of our support – trustworthy, personal and free of charge. Contact English Site The Federal Employment Agency's statistics and labour market reporting offers a wide range of services, from regularly published publications to special analyses. On our English site we provide the key figures on the German labour market, which are updated monthly, as well as a report on the European labour market situation.
